The urban settlement pattern, specifically the combination of mid-century apartment blocks and older stone masonry, is highly characteristic of Mediterranean coastal cities in Southern France. The presence of palm trees in the distance suggests a subtropical coastal climate typical of the Côte d'Azur.

The high density of multi-story residential buildings on a narrow street leading toward a port (indicated by the 'le Port' sign) is consistent with the geography of Monaco. The terrain is steeply sloped toward the sea, which is visible at the end of the street.
The architectural style and Mediterranean climate are similar to coastal regions like Catalonia, though the specific street layout and signage are more indicative of French-influenced regions.

The street lamp design (curved grey metal pole with a specific bracket) and the style of the white roller shutters (volets roulants) on the windows are extremely characteristic of French urban architecture. Additionally, the specific style of the small, white rectangular street nameplate mounted on the lamp post is a common French municipal standard.
